problem.title: Valid Parentheses
problem.description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if: Open brackets must be closed by the same type of brackets. Open brackets must be closed in the correct order.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Stack
solutions.description: A stack stores each opening bracket as it is encountered; when a closing bracket is seen, the top of the stack must be its matching opener, otherwise the string is invalid. An empty stack at the end confirms every opener was properly closed. The key insight is that the most recently opened bracket must always be closed first — a LIFO structure mirrors this nesting constraint exactly.
